summary: Implementation-ready engineering plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function. Core application logic (trapi_client.py, blob_writer.py, validator.py, function_app.py), unit and integration tests, Key Vault/monitoring/alerts Bicep modules, and all five GitHub Actions workflows are largely implemented. Key remaining gaps are: (1) no top-level Bicep that provisions the App Service Plan, host storage, and data storage accounts from scratch — main.bicep assumes a pre-existing Function App; (2) TRAPI ChatCompletion response parsing is broken — trapi_client.py validates the raw ChatCompletion envelope instead of the parsed message content; (3) production slot RBAC for Storage Blob Data Contributor is missing; (4) CI workflow is not disabled by default as required by spec; (5) TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME and TRAPI_API_VERSION are absent from functionapp.bicep app settings; (6) no missed-execution alert rule; and (7) staging slot has no Key Vault Secrets User RBAC.
assumptions: TRAPI endpoint https://trapi.research.microsoft.com/gcr/shared is reachable over public internet from the Azure Consumption Plan function app without VNet or private endpoint configuration.
The GPT-4o deployment name (TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME) will be confirmed and supplied by the owner (rciapala) before the integration test can pass end-to-end.
Zone-redundant Key Vault (Premium SKU) is supported in the target Azure region; the existing zoneRedundancyRegionConfirmed guard parameter confirms this.
The bootstrap identity used in cd-setup.yml already has Microsoft Graph permissions to create app registrations and federated identity credentials.

Soft-delete and versioned blobs in the spec are satisfied by the 7-day blob soft-delete setting already wired into storage provisioning (per infra memories). No separate blob versioning policy is required unless specified.
The staging and production environments each have separate dedicated data storage accounts, each provisioned by the CD Infra workflow.
Application Insights sampling is already configured in host.json and the monitoring.bicep module; no additional sampling tuning is required.
The CI workflow intentional non-disable (currently runs on push/PR) is a spec non-compliance and must be corrected to workflow_dispatch only.
open_questions: What is the confirmed TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME (GPT-4o model version) to pin in the prompt and app settings? The local.settings.json.example references 'gpt-5.2_2025-12-11' but this should be confirmed with the TRAPI team.
Does TRAPI support OpenAI response_format: {type: 'json_object'} or JSON schema enforcement? If so, it should be added to the request payload to prevent free-text responses that fail validation.
Is there a separate Azure subscription or resource group for staging vs production, or do both share the same resource group with environment-scoped variables in GitHub Actions?
Should the App Service Plan be shared across staging and production Function App slots, or are separate Consumption Plans required?
Is the TRAPI fallback credential (Key Vault secret 'trapi-fallback-credential') a static API key or token, and who seeds it into Key Vault post-provisioning?
Should the missed-execution alert (useMonitor) route through the same email action group as the other alerts, or to a separate on-call channel?
What is the intended behavior if the same-day blob already exists when the function is manually re-triggered? The current conditional PUT silently skips — should this log a warning or metric?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Close all infrastructure provisioning gaps so that all Azure resources can be created from scratch via a single CD Infra run, and fix the critical ChatCompletion response parsing bug that would cause every production run to fail.
entry_criteria: Repository is accessible and CI baseline is known.
Azure subscription, tenant, and resource group targets are confirmed.
TRAPI deployment name and endpoint are confirmed by owner.
exit_criteria: Full initial provisioning Bicep (App Service Plan, host storage, data storage accounts) is merged and passes az bicep build.
Production slot RBAC for Storage Blob Data Contributor is wired into Bicep.
TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME and TRAPI_API_VERSION appear in functionapp.bicep app settings.
Staging slot Key Vault Secrets User RBAC is assigned in Bicep.
trapi_client.py correctly parses choices[0].message.content before validation.
CI workflow trigger updated to workflow_dispatch only (disabled by default).
All existing unit tests continue to pass.

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Add deterministic JSON output enforcement, missed-execution alerting, Key Vault secret seeding documentation, and full test coverage for the fixed response parsing path.
entry_criteria: Foundation wave exit criteria are met.
TRAPI ChatCompletion parsing fix is merged and unit-tested.
exit_criteria: TRAPI request payload includes response_format or system prompt enforcing JSON schema output.
Missed timer execution alert rule is added to alerts.bicep.
Key Vault secret seeding procedure is documented and executable.
Unit tests cover ChatCompletion content extraction, JSON parse failure, and malformed choices array.
All unit tests pass with coverage >= 80%.

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Validate the full system end-to-end in staging, confirm all three alert rules fire under synthetic failure conditions, and gate the integration test into the promote workflow.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria are met.
Staging environment is provisioned via CD Infra.
CD App has deployed to staging slot.
exit_criteria: Integration test (test_integration.py) passes against staging, confirming Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson in output blob.
All three Application Insights alert rules (execution failure, duration, data quality) are verified active in the Azure portal.
Missed-execution alert is visible and enabled in Azure portal.
CD Promote workflow optionally invokes integration-staging.yml as a pre-promote gate.
Secret scan (gitleaks) passes on all branches with no findings.

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Deploy to production via staging slot swap, confirm monitoring is live, and publish complete operational documentation.
entry_criteria: Hardening wave exit criteria are met.
exit_criteria: Production slot is live via CD Promote slot swap.
Post-swap smoke test passes.
All alert rules are active in production Application Insights.
README documents full infrastructure bootstrap sequence including cd-setup → cd-infra → cd-app → cd-promote.
M1–M4 milestones are closed.

tasks.title: Create initial provisioning Bicep for App Service Plan, host storage, and data storage accounts
tasks.description: The current main.bicep assumes the Function App already exists and only handles secondary provisioning. Create a bootstrap Bicep template (e.g., infra/bootstrap.bicep) that provisions: Consumption App Service Plan (Y1/Dynamic), host storage account (Standard_LRS, identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage), production data storage account (Standard_LRS, Cool tier, soft-delete 7 days), and staging data storage account. Wire functionapp.bicep as a module from bootstrap.bicep so a single 'az deployment group create' on infra/bootstrap.bicep produces all required Azure resources from scratch.
inputs: Azure Functions Consumption Plan Bicep reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/bicep/resource-declaration
outputs: infra/bootstrap.bicep — top-level orchestrator deploying App Service Plan, host storage, data storage (prod + staging), and Function App module
infra/modules/storage.bicep — reusable storage account module with soft-delete, Cool tier, and allowSharedKeyAccess:false
infra/bootstrap.json — compiled ARM template artifact
acceptance_criteria: az bicep build --file infra/bootstrap.bicep succeeds with no errors.
bootstrap.bicep provisions App Service Plan with kind=functionapp and sku Y1/Dynamic.
Host storage account uses AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (identity-based, allowSharedKeyAccess:false).
Separate production and staging data storage accounts are provisioned with Standard_LRS, Cool tier, and blob soft-delete 7 days.
functionapp.bicep module is referenced from bootstrap.bicep so the Function App and staging slot are created in a single deployment.
CD Infra workflow (cd-infra.yml) can be pointed at bootstrap.bicep for initial environment provisioning without pre-existing resources.
risks: Consumption Plan name uniqueness constraints in the target region may require parameterization.
Storage account name length limit (24 chars) with uniqueString may truncate; validate naming.
Splitting into bootstrap.bicep vs main.bicep may require CD Infra workflow update to select the correct template file per invocation type.

tasks.title: Add Storage Blob Data Contributor RBAC assignment for production Function App slot
tasks.description: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep currently assigns Storage Blob Data Contributor only to the staging slot identity, not to the production slot (main Function App) identity. Add a role assignment resource scoped to the production data storage account container so the production slot can write blobs using Managed Identity.
inputs: spec.md — Access and Permissions: Storage Blob Data Contributor on yankees-roster container
Azure RBAC role assignment Bicep docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/role-assignments-bicep
outputs: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep — updated with productionSlotStorageBlobDataContributorRoleAssignment resource scoped to the production data storage account
acceptance_criteria: functionapp.bicep contains a role assignment for the production Function App identity (functionApp.identity.principalId) with Storage Blob Data Contributor on the production data storage account.
az bicep build passes with no errors after the change.
CD Infra validation step confirms RBAC assignment is present for the production slot after deployment.
Staging slot role assignment is unchanged and still present.
risks: Container-scoped vs account-scoped RBAC — spec requires container-scoped; ensure scope is /blobServices/default/containers/yankees-roster not the account root.
RBAC propagation delay (up to 15 minutes) may cause false negatives in CD Infra validation; the existing retry loop mitigates this.

tasks.title: Add TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME and TRAPI_API_VERSION to functionapp.bicep app settings
tasks.description: functionapp.bicep only includes TRAPI_ENDPOINT in app settings. TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME and TRAPI_API_VERSION are required env vars consumed by trapi_client.py but are missing from both the production and staging slot app settings blocks. Add parameters and app settings entries for both slots.
inputs: trapi_client.py — TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME and TRAPI_API_VERSION env var reads
outputs: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep — updated with trapiDeploymentName and trapiApiVersion parameters and app settings entries for both production and staging slots
infra/modules/functionapp.json — recompiled ARM JSON
acceptance_criteria: functionapp.bicep accepts trapiDeploymentName and trapiApiVersion as parameters.
Both production and staging slot appSettings arrays include TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME and TRAPI_API_VERSION entries.
TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME is added to slotStickySettings.appSettingNames so staging and production can use different model versions independently.
az bicep build passes after the change.
CD Infra deployment surfaces both settings in az functionapp config appsettings list output.
risks: Pinning different TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME values per slot requires the deployer to supply both values at provision time; document required parameters in README.

tasks.title: Add Key Vault Secrets User RBAC for staging slot managed identity
tasks.description: keyvault.bicep grants Key Vault Secrets User only to the production Function App identity. The staging slot has its own system-assigned identity and also reads Key Vault references (TRAPI_FALLBACK_CREDENTIAL, APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING). Add a role assignment in keyvault.bicep for the staging slot principal ID so Key Vault references resolve in the staging slot.
inputs: Azure Functions deployment slots Key Vault references: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references
outputs: infra/modules/keyvault.bicep — updated with stagingSlotPrincipalId parameter and stagingSlotKeyVaultSecretsUserRoleAssignment resource
infra/modules/keyvault.json — recompiled
acceptance_criteria: keyvault.bicep accepts an optional stagingSlotPrincipalId parameter.
When stagingSlotPrincipalId is non-empty, a Key Vault Secrets User role assignment is created for the staging slot identity.
main.bicep passes the staging slot principal ID from functionapp.bicep outputs to the keyvault module.
az bicep build passes after the change.
After CD Infra deployment, az keyvault show confirms both identities have access.
risks: main.bicep currently references an existing Function App and its principal ID; it will need to be updated to also accept the staging slot principal ID output from bootstrap.bicep.

tasks.title: Fix TRAPI ChatCompletion response parsing in trapi_client.py
tasks.description: trapi_client.py calls validate_roster_response(response_json) on the raw ChatCompletion envelope. The TRAPI/OpenAI API returns {choices:[{message:{content:'<JSON string>'}}]} not {players:[...]}. The code must extract choices[0].message.content, JSON-parse it, and pass the parsed object to validate_roster_response. The raw ChatCompletion envelope should be used only for token_count extraction and for writing to the failed/ blob on parse failure.
outputs: trapi_client.py — updated fetch_1985_yankees_roster to extract and JSON-parse choices[0].message.content before validation; raises RosterValidationError with the raw envelope as response_payload on JSON parse failure
acceptance_criteria: fetch_1985_yankees_roster extracts response_json['choices'][0]['message']['content'] and json.loads it before passing to validate_roster_response.
If choices is missing, empty, or content is not valid JSON, a RosterValidationError is raised with kind=SCHEMA_FAILURE and response_payload set to the raw ChatCompletion envelope.
token_count continues to be extracted from response_json['usage']['total_tokens'] (the envelope level).
The blob written to yankees-roster/{date}.json contains the parsed player roster object, not the ChatCompletion envelope.
All existing unit tests are updated and pass with the new parsing logic.
New unit tests cover: missing choices key, empty choices array, non-JSON content string, valid ChatCompletion with well-formed roster.
risks: The fake TRAPI responses in all existing unit tests return {players:[...]} directly — they must all be updated to wrap the payload in the ChatCompletion envelope structure, which is a broad change across test_trapi_client.py.
If TRAPI streams responses (stream:true), the parsing logic is different; confirm with TRAPI team that streaming is not used.

tasks.title: Disable CI workflow by default per spec (workflow_dispatch only)
tasks.description: The spec states CI is 'Disabled by default'. The current ci.yml triggers on push to main and on all pull_request branches, which is non-compliant. Update ci.yml to use workflow_dispatch as the only trigger, removing the push and pull_request triggers.
inputs: spec.md — Deployment Model: CI Disabled by default
outputs: .github/workflows/ci.yml — updated with on: workflow_dispatch only
acceptance_criteria: ci.yml on: block contains only workflow_dispatch.
No push or pull_request triggers remain in ci.yml.
The workflow can still be invoked manually from the GitHub Actions UI.
The secret-scan and pytest jobs are unchanged and continue to pass when manually triggered.
risks: Removing the PR trigger means no automatic test gate on pull requests; developers must manually trigger CI before merging, which requires team process awareness.

tasks.title: Add JSON response_format enforcement to TRAPI request payload
tasks.description: The TRAPI request payload currently sends only a user message without enforcing structured JSON output. Add response_format: {type: 'json_object'} to the payload (if supported by TRAPI) and/or a system message that reinforces the JSON schema, to reduce the chance of free-text responses that fail schema validation. Confirm TRAPI supports response_format with the TRAPI team before merging.
inputs: TRAPI team confirmation on response_format support
outputs: trapi_client.py — updated payload with response_format: {type: 'json_object'} and/or system message
prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt — optionally updated with system-role prompt for structured output
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI payload includes response_format: {type: 'json_object'} when the API version supports it.
A system message is included that reinforces the expected JSON schema.
Unit tests verify the payload includes response_format and messages contains the system role entry.
fetch_1985_yankees_roster still validates the parsed content against the schema regardless of response_format enforcement.
risks: TRAPI may not support response_format at all API versions; gate this on TRAPI team confirmation to avoid a 400 Bad Request in production.
response_format: {type: 'json_object'} requires at least one message to contain the word 'json'; the current prompt already mentions JSON schema so this is likely satisfied.

tasks.title: Add missed timer execution alert rule to alerts.bicep
tasks.description: The spec requires an alert when the nightly timer trigger execution is missed. While useMonitor:true is configured, there is no Application Insights alert rule that fires when zero executions are recorded in the expected daily window. Add a Scheduled Query Rule to alerts.bicep that fires when the function has not executed at least once in a 26-hour window.
inputs: spec.md — Monitoring: missed executions surfaced via Application Insights alert
outputs: infra/modules/alerts.bicep — missedExecutionAlert Scheduled Query Rule resource checking for zero function completions in a 26-hour window
infra/modules/alerts.json — recompiled
acceptance_criteria: alerts.bicep contains a missedExecutionAlert Scheduled Query Rule that queries requests or customEvents for function_completed events.
Alert fires when count of completions in a 26-hour window is less than 1 (threshold: 0, operator: LessThanOrEqual using Count aggregation).
Alert severity is 1 (critical) and routes to the existing email action group.
az bicep build passes after the change.
Alert rule is visible and enabled in Azure portal after CD Infra deployment.
risks: The function emits function_completed as a log event (ILogger.Info), not as a custom metric; the KQL query must target traces or customEvents table, not customMetrics.
A 26-hour window accommodates up to 2 hours of clock drift or cold-start delay without a false positive.

tasks.title: Document and automate Key Vault secret seeding for TRAPI fallback credential
tasks.description: The Key Vault reference TRAPI_FALLBACK_CREDENTIAL points to a secret named 'trapi-fallback-credential' in Key Vault. If this secret is absent, the Key Vault reference will fail to resolve and the Function App will fail to start. Document the secret seeding procedure and optionally add a one-time seeding script or az keyvault secret set step to cd-infra.yml guarded by a workflow input flag.
inputs: spec.md — Secrets: Azure Key Vault stores TRAPI credentials
outputs: README.md — new section 'Key Vault secret seeding' with az keyvault secret set command and required permissions
Optional: infra/seed-secrets.sh — idempotent script to seed the fallback credential secret (value placeholder)
acceptance_criteria: README contains a 'Key Vault secret seeding' section with the exact az keyvault secret set command and required RBAC role (Key Vault Secrets Officer).
The procedure is idempotent (az keyvault secret set --if-notexist or equivalent).
The seeded secret value is documented as a placeholder and never committed to source control.
After seeding, az keyvault secret show confirms the secret exists and the Function App app settings show the Key Vault reference as Resolved.
risks: If the TRAPI fallback credential is not needed (Managed Identity is sufficient for TRAPI auth), the secret can be seeded with a placeholder value to prevent Key Vault reference resolution failure.
Secret value must not be stored in the repository or workflow logs; use masked workflow secrets or manual seeding.

tasks.title: Update unit tests to cover ChatCompletion response parsing and edge cases
tasks.description: After APP-01 fixes the ChatCompletion parsing, all existing unit tests that mock a flat {players:[...]} response must be updated to wrap the mock response in the ChatCompletion envelope structure. New test cases must cover: missing choices key, empty choices array, choices[0].message.content is not valid JSON, and content is valid JSON but fails schema validation.
outputs: tests/test_trapi_client.py — updated mock responses wrapped in ChatCompletion envelope; new test cases for parsing edge cases
coverage.xml — updated coverage report showing trapi_client parse path >= 80%
acceptance_criteria: All existing test_trapi_client.py tests pass after updating mock responses to ChatCompletion envelope format.
test_fetch_roster_raises_validation_error_on_missing_choices verifies RosterValidationError is raised when choices key is absent.
test_fetch_roster_raises_validation_error_on_non_json_content verifies RosterValidationError is raised when content is not valid JSON.
test_fetch_roster_raises_validation_error_on_malformed_players verifies schema failure path is preserved.
python -m pytest tests/ -v -m 'not integration' --cov-fail-under=80 passes.
risks: Updating all existing mocked responses is a broad change; carefully verify that retry, backoff, and fatal-status-code tests still exercise the correct code paths after the envelope change.

tasks.title: Wire integration test as pre-promote gate in CD Promote workflow
tasks.description: The integration-staging.yml workflow exists as a standalone dispatch but is not automatically invoked before a staging-to-production slot swap. Update cd-promote.yml to optionally call integration-staging.yml as a prerequisite job (or document the manual gate), ensuring that known player assertions pass against the staging blob before the swap proceeds.
outputs: .github/workflows/cd-promote.yml — updated with an optional integration test job (guarded by workflow input flag run_integration_tests: true/false) that must pass before the swap job
acceptance_criteria: cd-promote.yml accepts a boolean input run_integration_tests (default: true).
When run_integration_tests is true, an integration job runs test_integration.py against the staging slot before the swap step.
The swap step has needs: [integration] so it is blocked until the integration job succeeds.
When run_integration_tests is false, the workflow proceeds directly to the smoke test and swap.
Workflow runs without requiring additional Azure CLI permissions beyond what is already granted.
risks: Integration test invokes the function and waits up to 240 seconds for blob output, which may significantly increase the promote workflow wall-clock time.
The integration test requires RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true and DATA_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME to be available as environment secrets/vars in the staging environment.

tasks.title: Deploy to staging and validate end-to-end with integration test
tasks.description: Run the full staging deployment sequence: cd-infra (bootstrap.bicep for initial provisioning if first deploy, or main.bicep for incremental), then cd-app targeting staging slot, then integration-staging.yml. Confirm the output blob in staging contains Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson with a player count of 24–28.
inputs: Staging environment secrets and variables configured in GitHub
outputs: Staging blob yankees-roster/{date}.json containing valid 1985 Yankees roster
GitHub Actions workflow run artifacts confirming green integration test
Application Insights showing function_completed log event for the staging run
acceptance_criteria: CD Infra workflow completes with successful Bicep deployment and RBAC/container validation.
CD App workflow deploys to staging slot and smoke test returns HTTP 200/202/204.
Integration test passes: blob contains Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, Rickey Henderson; player count 24–28.
Application Insights shows function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed log events.
player_count_returned custom metric appears in Application Insights customMetrics table.
No Key Vault reference resolution errors in Function App configuration.
risks: TRAPI endpoint rate limits or quota exhaustion during staging validation could cause the integration test to fail transiently.
Cold-start latency on Consumption Plan may cause the first post-deploy function invocation to time out on the smoke test; the existing 6-attempt retry loop mitigates this.

tasks.title: Promote staging to production via slot swap and confirm post-swap smoke test
tasks.description: Run cd-promote.yml with source-slot=staging and target-slot=production (with run_integration_tests=true). Confirm the pre-swap smoke test, slot swap, post-swap version verification, and post-swap smoke test all pass. Confirm all alert rules are active in the production Application Insights instance.
inputs: Production environment secrets and variables configured in GitHub
outputs: Production Function App serving the validated roster function
GitHub Actions CD Promote run summary with confirmed promoted version marker
Application Insights alert rules confirmed active in Azure portal
acceptance_criteria: CD Promote workflow completes with pre-swap smoke test HTTP 200/202/204 on staging slot.
Slot swap succeeds and post-swap version marker on production matches pre-swap source marker.
Post-swap smoke test on production returns HTTP 200/202/204.
All three alert rules (execution failure, duration, data quality) plus missed execution alert are enabled in production Application Insights.
Function App configuration shows all Key Vault references as Resolved.
Production slot is running the same code version that passed integration test in staging.
risks: Consumption Plan production slot may have a longer cold start than staging; post-swap smoke test uses a 6-attempt retry loop to mitigate.
WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE or SCM_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE marker must be present in both staging and production for the promote workflow version verification to succeed.

tasks.title: Document full infrastructure bootstrap sequence and environment variable matrix
tasks.description: The README currently documents secondary provisioning (cd-infra with main.bicep) but not the initial bootstrap sequence using the new bootstrap.bicep. Add a section describing the full first-time provisioning sequence (cd-setup → cd-infra with bootstrap.bicep → cd-infra with main.bicep → cd-app → cd-promote) and a table of all required GitHub environment variables and secrets per environment.
inputs: All CD workflow inputs and required vars/secrets
outputs: README.md — new 'Initial environment bootstrap' section with step-by-step sequence and a GitHub environment vars/secrets reference table (AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP, AZURE_FUNCTIONAPP_NAME, STAGING_TRAPI_ENDPOINT, STAGING_TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, DATA_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, etc.)
acceptance_criteria: README contains an 'Initial environment bootstrap' section that references bootstrap.bicep and explains when to use it vs main.bicep.
A table lists every required GitHub Actions environment variable and secret, which workflow reads it, and whether it is a var or secret.
The Key Vault secret seeding step is cross-referenced from the bootstrap sequence.
The TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME confirmation requirement is called out as a prerequisite before running cd-infra.
risks: Documentation can become stale if Bicep parameters change; consider adding a comment in bootstrap.bicep that cross-references README.
